actions.detail: role 'Claims Analyst' bound to permission group
role 'Scheduling & Delay Specialist' bound to permission group
role 'Contract Administrator' bound to permission group
role 'Claims Review Board Manager' bound to permission group
role 'Project Controls Auditor' bound to permission group

deferred.reason: Claims-domain policies (no entitlement position communicated to a claimant before the human determination; every rejection must cite the governing contract clause and the record evidence relied upon; no surety default declaration without contract-required notice and cure period; negotiated settlements above authority thresholds escalate to the review board) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route final determinations exclusively to human analysts with the Claims Review Board Manager as approver.

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (Claim Intake Copilot, Delay Analysis Assistant for TIA/windows narratives, Quantum Review Builder with measured-mile benchmarking, Notice & Deadline Sentinel) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (project-controls and PMIS platforms: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, Oracle Primavera P6 and Unifier, e-Builder, Kahua; ERP/cost systems: CMiC, Sage 300 CRE, Viewpoint Vista; document management for daily reports, RFIs, submittals and boring logs) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (execute a change order in the contract/ERP system, post a backcharge, release or hold a progress payment, issue a formal claim determination letter, transmit a surety notice) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with human approval — no autonomous change-order execution, payment movement, or default declaration, ever.

Golden eval sets (entitlement triage, delay-analysis method selection, quantum reasonableness ranking, notice-timeliness screening) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Project, Contract, Party, Surety, Claim, ChangeOrder, DelayEvent, ScheduleUpdate, NoticeRecord, Backcharge, PayApplication, DeterminationLetter) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the construction_claims_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (change_order_review, delay_claim_analysis, dsc_investigation, defect_backcharge_review, payment_dispute_review, acceleration_claim_review) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (claim_outcome_confidence, quantum_inflation_score) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest contract-book anomaly + xgboost claim-outcome scorer) with model-governance expectations noted in grounding memories.

Display-label overlays ("Claim", "Change order", "Notice deadline at risk", "Surety notice pending", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
